danger_signals.visual_form: Large colored humanoid robot sprite with distinctive silhouette (Cut Man's scissors head, Fire Man's flame body, Ice Man's cold aesthetic, Bomb Man's explosive form, Elec Man's electrical appearance, Guts Man's powerful build)
danger_signals.meaning: Boss encounter imminent; stage culmination; significant threat requiring specific weapon vulnerability

danger_signals.visual_form: Kamadoma (standing upright or throwing), Pickelman (with shield), Watcher, and other stage-specific creatures with distinct animations
danger_signals.meaning: Combat hazard; obstacle to navigate; health/ammo depletion risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Repeated sectors with escalating complexity, flat plains, solid obstacles, multi-screen progression (down 1, right 8, into boss chamber pattern)
danger_signals.meaning: Elevated difficulty; culmination stage; final confrontation approaching; world-state shift to Wily's domain

danger_signals.visual_form: Top-left corner health bar decreasing in length
danger_signals.meaning: Mega Man vulnerability increasing; potential game-over state approaching

safety_signals.visual_form: Small collectible sprites dropped by defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Health restoration available; player survival resource; indicates progress against enemy

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items scattered from enemy defeats
safety_signals.meaning: Special weapon ammunition replenishment; resource availability; sustained combat capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual transition, score display for Robot Master defeat (50,000-100,000 points)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage achieved; stage progression unlocked; special weapon acquired; temporary safety milestone

safety_signals.visual_form: Special item located in Elec Man's stage
safety_signals.meaning: Environmental navigation aid; safe passage tool; accessibility facilitator for reaching otherwise inaccessible areas

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mega Man humanoid robot form; Rock identity (Rock converted to fighting robot)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dr. Light's helper designation; appears in narrative context; associated with justice and protection against Wily

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Six Robot Masters (Cut Man, Fire Man, Ice Man, Bomb Man, Elec Man, Guts Man); Wily Stage factory architecture; Copy Robot boss (identical behavior to Elec Man in Wily Stage 2)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Robot Master variants: Color-coded bodies reflecting their function (Fire = warm palette, Ice = cool palette, Elec = electrical visual effects, Guts = industrial heavy build, Cut = blade-focused design, Bomb = explosive form)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Wily's robot factory (Pacific-based location); Wily Stages 1-4 architecture; repeated sector patterns; flat plains boss arenas; manufacturing facility design

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Six Robot Masters under Wily control; visually identical to Light's originals but behaviorally hostile
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Robot Masters display combat-ready stances and attack animations; Copy Robot demonstrates perfect mimicry of Elec Man with unused jump behavior when obstacles present
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Robot Master stages (six distinct environments); stage-specific enemy placements; boss chambers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six Robot Master stage icons displayed; player free to choose order; stage select menu centered with Wily stage hidden until all six are cleared; nonlinear gameplay presented visually through menu structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mega Man awaits player input; no active threats; enemies dormant until stage entry; world in state of preparation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage-specific background art and tileset (Cut Man's industrial setting, Fire Man's thermal environment, Ice Man's frozen landscape, Bomb Man's explosive arena, Elec Man's electrical zone, Guts Man's powerful terrain); scrolling environment; enemy placements relative to hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies engage in patrol and attack patterns; Robot Master executes signature attack animations; Mega Man must navigate stage-specific obstacles; progressive difficulty through stage depth

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score display (50,000-100,000 points) for defeated Master; stage clear confirmation; special weapon visual added to arsenal; stage becomes revisitable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Wily's control weakens on that robot type; stage becomes safe to traverse without boss threat; narrative progression; one of six master objectives completed

world_state_to_visual.world_state: All six Robot Masters defeated (Wily Stage unlocked)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage select menu now displays Wily Stage 7 in center position (previously hidden); visual confirmation of world-state shift; environmental transition from individual robot domains to unified Wily factory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final confrontation becomes possible; multi-stage factory progression required; escalated enemy complexity and boss replicas appear; Dr. Wily directly threatens world

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Factory architecture with repeated sector patterns; elemental hazards; flat plains with solid obstacles; Copy Robot arena (identical to Elec Man visually but in Wily context)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Replica Robot Masters encountered; Copy Robot mimics Elec Man behavior; escalated difficulty; player weapon arsenal now essential for progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Further factory penetration; final boss chambers; architectural complexity increases; multiple boss encounters in sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final robot defenders encountered; Dr. Wily showdown approaches; player faces custom-designed Wily robots (not copies); climactic confrontation imminent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score display for Wily victory (200,000 points - highest scoring enemy); ending sequence triggered; visual narrative conclusion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: World threat eliminated; Mega Man returns home; family reunion ending; world-state reset to safety; heroic narrative completion

environmental_storytelling.technique: Stage thematic design through visual aesthetics
environmental_storytelling.example: Cut Man's stage features scissor/blade visual motifs; Fire Man's domain uses warm color palette and flame imagery; Ice Man's environment displays frozen aesthetics; Bomb Man's arena shows explosive visual language; Elec Man's stage exhibits electrical visual effects; Guts Man's terrain displays industrial power and weight - each stage's visual identity communicates the Robot Master's core function and threat type without explicit text

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss chamber architecture as narrative beat
environmental_storytelling.example: Transition from stage navigation to flat arena signals boss encounter is imminent; repeated architectural patterns in Wily stages communicate factory setting and industrial threat scale; multi-room factory progression visually escalates threat magnitude

Specific enemy types positioned in stage sectors communicate environmental hazards (Kamadoma, Pickelman, Watcher each thematically match their stage); enemy density increases toward boss chamber, escalating danger narrative through visual placement

environmental_storytelling.technique: Health and ammo resource visibility as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Energy cell and ammunition drops visible after enemy defeat communicate that player survival is possible; resource frequency indicates stage difficulty and remaining threats; depletion of resources signals stage culmination approaching

environmental_storytelling.technique: Special item placement (Magnet Beam in Elec Man's stage)
environmental_storytelling.example: Unique navigation tool embedded in specific stage environment communicates level design intent and provides environmental puzzle solution; item location teaches player about stage verticality and optional progression paths

environmental_storytelling.technique: Wily Stage visual continuity through repeated sectors
environmental_storytelling.example: Recycled Elec Man stage geometry repurposed in Wily's factory (documented unused level data showing sector repetition) communicates that Wily has appropriated Light's designs; visual redundancy with escalating complexity tells story of Wily's industrial scale and resourcefulness

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Robot Master (Cut Man, Fire Man, Ice Man, Bomb Man, Elec Man, Guts Man)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Boss-specific attack patterns; Cut Man uses blade attacks; Fire Man deploys flame projectiles; Ice Man fires ice attacks; Bomb Man throws explosives; Elec Man conducts electrical assaults; Guts Man executes earthquake/stomp attacks; each displays signature animation set communicating their unique threat and weapon vulnerability

Demonstrates Elec Man behavior in Wily context; uses Elec Man's jump script when blocked by obstacles (unused behavior accessible if room contains solid obstacles); can trigger mid-air jumping when player input occurs during fall state; perfect mimicry communicates Wily's copying capability and technological mastery
Standing upright patrol (unused sprite variant exists showing pre-jump squat position); likely throws or attacks; stage-specific placement communicates environmental threat type
Throwing attack with shield; shield position shifts one pixel forward in unused variant; shield indicates defensive characteristic; throwing behavior communicates ranged threat
Behavioral documentation not provided in sources; unknown tiles suggest distinctive appearance
Energy cell or ammunition cell drop occurs at random; dropped items remain until collected or stage exit; visual indicator that enemy provided resource; encourages exploration and combat engagement

Unused Game Genie code (PEXPIIAA) reveals Mega Man can be stunned depending on hit direction (Guts Man earthquake mechanic originally); would have communicated directional damage system in original design; mechanic removal simplified final feedback
Confronted in Pacific-based robot factory; multiple defeat phases (replicas, custom robots, Wily himself); awards 200,000 points (highest score) communicating ultimate threat significance

notes: Mega Man uses nonlinear stage selection to allow player agency in world-state progression. Visual identity is primarily communicated through color-coded Robot Master designs and stage thematic aesthetics rather than explicit narrative elements. The game's manifestation layer relies on enemy placement, environmental design, and sprite animation to communicate threat levels and world state. Regional differences exist (Western plot altered to include 'Monsteropolis' location and changed character relationships) but core visual language remains consistent. Unused content reveals original design intent for directional stun mechanics and more complex Robot Master animations (Guts Man squat, Super Cutter spherical body design) suggesting iterative visual communication refinement during development.
